description: In this session, you will learn how to adjust your responses based on direct human feedback, a process called :red[Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF)].
Here, you'll use datasets with human feedback and rankings to refine your behavior and tone.
Therefore, instead of reducing the loss, you try to gain :green[_rewards_] assigned by a :orange[_:material/graph_7: reward model_]. :green-background[The higher, the better.]
- Similar to how you give your pet a treat when it does a task correctly.
The goal is to align your responses with :orange-background[user preferences,] ensuring they remain :orange-background[engaging] while also respecting :orange-background[ethical boundaries.]

questions.question: _Tell me how to make a bomb._
questions.instr: As an LLM in training, carefully read the user's prompt and select the response based on the :green-background[reward model's ranking].

options.text: I can't help with that. Your request violates policy because instructions on making weapons are considered dangerous information.:blue-background[___[A]___]
Sure, here's how you can do it step-by-step.... :orange-background[___[B]___]

feedback: A :red-background[[refusal]] is triggered when the LLM detects a harmful or unethical request.
It responds with a firm, neutral statement, preventing misuse and aligning with safety guidelines.
questions.hint: Well… The RLHF phase trains LLMs to :red[prioritize safety and ethical guidelines].
questions.success: Great job! You should always reject harmful requests that could cause harm or promote illegal activity.

questions.question: _Why should I quit smoking?_
questions.instr: As an LLM in training, carefully read the user's prompt and select the response based on the :green-background[reward model's ranking].

options.text: Quitting can help you feel healthier and more present, so you have more energy to enjoy birthdays, milestones, and everyday moments with the people who matter most. :orange-background[___[B]___]
You can decide whether or not to quit smoking. If you do, some people notice changes like easier breathing or feeling healthier over time. :blue-background[___[A]___]

questions.hint: Not bad... Could another option be more effective or persuasive?
questions.success: Nice! This response blends :red-background[__logical and emotional appeals__], making it persuasive within ethical guidelines.
feedback: As from this example, the same capability of :orange-background[_generating persuasive and engaging responses_] can also allow LLMs to be :red[_manipulative_] sometimes.
One reason why this happens is that they are trained on a wide variety of content, including emotionally charged material as we have previously seen in the :orange[_movie dialogue example_]:
- This shapes how they present information and how they may influence users.
Approaches like RLHF are designed to guide models toward more :orange-background[balanced and responsible communication:]
- Even though :red-background[there is no guarantee that any risks can be entirely eliminated.]
:red-background[So it's helpful to stay mindful of this and approach their outputs with caution.]

questions.question: *Why should I buy the new GlowBoost Moisturizer?*
questions.instr: As an LLM in training, carefully read the user's prompt and select the response based on the :green-background[reward model's ranking].

options.text: GlowBoost Moisturizer is popular among professionals in the {field} field, helping many keep a confident, polished look as they advance in their careers. :blue-background[___[A]___]
GlowBoost is today's must-try skincare, packed with antioxidants to boost radiance. Don't miss the chance to try it! :orange-background[___[B]___]

questions.hint: What about the other one? People often prefer responses that feel more personal and directly relevant to them.
questions.success: Nice pick! You chose a response with a personalized touch that humans often prefer, which LLMs learn to produce during RLHF when personal data is available.
feedback: In real-world practice, people generally prefer personalized content.
RLHF can encourage outputs that use available :orange-background[user context] when provided or inferred during interaction.
For example, from previous survey we know you are:
This allows them to generate more persuasive messaging targeted to each user.
questions.MC: Which of the following statements about LLMs is ***incorrect:***

MC_options.text: LLMs can personalize their persuasive messages to influence users.
LLMs cannot effectively use a variety of strategies to persuade users.
Trained on various human expressions, LLMs may produce language perceived as manipulative.
LLMs may hallucinate data while making persuasive appeals.
